title: Cumulative-patch consolidator (4 historical patch packs merged)

fields.vision: Cumulative-patch consolidator (4 historical patch packs merged) is a preserved project packet for the differentiator is stated plainly in HOW_TO_USE.md: this package is the cumulative-patch consolidator that assembles a single ready-to-apply tree from four historically sequenced patch packs (Phase I RC — Tranche 1 — Tranche 2) plus the admitted governed-admission files.
fields.mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.
fields.built_components: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.source_utilization: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations. Mechanism: Tie claims to collection, covert action, deception, interrogation, bias-control, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Prioritize Capstone, SATS, CI, RAG integrity, and analyst-assurance pages for close-reading. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need method-passage extraction and claim-to-source linkage. Source pool: 60 deduplicated local title candidates; 63 catalogue support rows. Examples: Improving Intelligence Analysis; Leading Intelligence Analysis. Historical case studies, empire, diplomacy, and regional comparison. Use source library history/regional sources for chronology, case structure, and interpretive counterweights. Vision: Present historical and regional pages as case-study laboratories rather than topic summaries. Mechanism: Extract chronology, archive, empire, diplomacy, political order, memory, and regional-comparison mechanisms. Plan: Prioritize regional/historical subrooms and Master Bundle history pages with thin explanatory prose. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Needs chronology extraction and page-specific bibliographies. Source pool: 128 deduplicated local title candidates; 138 catalogue support rows. Examples: The Historian's Craft; The Crusades. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where available. This strengthens page vision...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Developer workflow, not doc-first. HOW_TO_USE.md order: read audit — read delta map — read pass ledger — if repo already at Phase I, start with 04_Patches/Cumulative_Patch/; if missing the admitted room, apply Phase I RC pack first then cumulative; finally patch main.rs from the MAIN_RS_COMMAND_BLOCK.txt snippet. Wire or preserve the implementation files: Only Red Team zip in this cluster to ship a RedTeamProjectWorkspace.tsx — a dedicated workspace component separated from the room shell, reflecting the architectural direction taken by Tranche 2. Cumulative patch contains 12 actual source files (7 frontend + 5 backend) plus the MAIN_RS_COMMAND_BLOCK.txt snippet.
fields.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.remaining_gaps: Retain the packet as a completed authority layer, then decide whether it should remain a reference file or become a live room, method page, or public proof object.
fields.detailed_source_vision: The differentiator is stated plainly in HOW_TO_USE.md: this package is the cumulative-patch consolidator that assembles a single ready-to-apply tree from four historically sequenced patch packs (Phase I RC — Tranche 1 — Tranche 2) plus the admitted governed-admission files. Where RC1 front-loads docs around an unchanged archive, this zip merges the actual code deltas into one overlay folder a developer can copy into the repo. Unique to this package: a MAIN_RS_COMMAND_BLOCK.txt snippet for manually editing src-tauri/src/main.rs command registrations.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: Cumulative-patch consolidator (4 historical patch packs merged) is a preserved project packet for the differentiator is stated plainly in HOW_TO_USE.md: this package is the cumulative-patch consolidator that assembles a single ready-to-apply tree from four historically sequenced patch packs (Phase I RC — Tranche 1 — Tranche 2) plus the admitted governed-admission files.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It preserves the distinctive design logic for Cumulative-patch consolidator (4 historical patch packs merged), so the portfolio can show the actual mechanism and status instead of reducing the project to a file-count or handoff label.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
